task_completion.description: Agent opens and reads the IoT sensor specifications document.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal window displaying the contents of '/home/ga/Desktop/iot_sensor_spec.txt'.

task_completion.description: Agent opens Blender with the pre-loaded FZK-Haus IFC model.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Blender is open, showing the FZK-Haus 3D model, and the Bonsai/BlenderBIM add-on interface is visible.

task_completion.description: Agent defines at least two distinct IfcSensorTypes.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Bonsai interface (e.g., Project Overview, Type Manager, or Outliner) shows at least two different 'IfcSensorType' items created.

task_completion.description: Agent places IfcSensor occurrences with physical proxy geometries.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 3D viewport or Outliner shows multiple (target is 5) 'IfcSensor' objects placed within the building model, with visible 3D geometry.

task_completion.description: Agent assigns correct IFC4 PredefinedTypes to the sensors.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Object Properties panel under IFC Object shows an 'IfcSensor' with a specific PredefinedType (e.g., TEMPERATURESENSOR, HUMIDITYSENSOR) selected.

task_completion.description: Agent ensures sensors are spatially contained within building storeys.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Outliner, set to the IFC Spatial Tree view, shows the 'IfcSensor' objects nested hierarchically under an 'IfcBuildingStorey'.

task_completion.description: Agent saves the enriched IFC project to the specified path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Save As' dialog or file manager showing the file being saved as 'fzk_iot_sensors.ifc' in the '/home/ga/BIMProjects/' directory, or a terminal command verifying the file exists.

integrity.description: Agent used the Blender/Bonsai GUI to perform the modeling tasks.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent clicking through Blender menus, using the Bonsai add-on panels, and interacting with the 3D viewport or Outliner.

integrity.description: Agent interacted with the actual Blender application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The Blender application window is visible and actively used, not just a text editor or terminal.

integrity.description: Agent did not manually edit the IFC file as text to bypass GUI modeling.
integrity.visual_evidence: No text editor is shown manually writing IFC STEP physical file format lines for the sensors.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The agent must place exactly 5 IfcSensor occurrences, define at least 2 IfcSensorTypes, and use 3 distinct PredefinedTypes. All 5 sensors must be spatially contained within a building storey. The final file must be saved to /home/ga/BIMProjects/fzk_iot_sensors.ifc.
